title: 10 Bargain-Bin Pickups That Outproduced a $211M Contract

slides.headline: Donovan Clingan — 10.0 WS, Portland +15 wins
slides.data_callout: $24.2M total. 3.7x more Win Shares than Paul George at 11% of the cost.

slides.headline: Neemias Queta — 9.2 WS for $5M in Boston
slides.data_callout: $545K per Win Share — second-best value of any signing since 2024.

slides.headline: Goga Bitadze — 9.0 WS for $15.9M in Orlando
slides.data_callout: $1.8M per WS vs. George's $78.4M per WS. A 43x gap.

slides.headline: Kel'el Ware — 8.9 WS, All-Rookie, Miami
slides.data_callout: $16.2M total. Miami's best value acquisition of the offseason.

slides.headline: Naji Marshall — 7.9 WS for $18.4M in Dallas
slides.data_callout: 2.9x more Win Shares than George at 8.7% of the cost.

slides.headline: Collin Gillespie — 6.1 WS on a two-way contract
slides.data_callout: $376K per Win Share — best value of any move since 2024.

slides.headline: Cam Spencer — 5.2 WS on a Memphis two-way
Jaylen Wells — 5.0 WS, All-Rookie, $1.2M salary
slides.data_callout: 33rd pick. Made All-Rookie. Costs what George earns every 3 games.

slides.headline: Ajay Mitchell — 5.2 WS, championship ring, OKC two-way
slides.data_callout: $11.7M total. NBA champion. Nearly 2x George's production.

slides.headline: Justin Champagnie — 5.3 WS, converted two-way, Washington
